title: Incidence rate of cardiovascular diseases
subtitle: Estimated share of the population who newly develop [cardiovascular diseases](#dod:cvd), in a given year.
note: To allow for comparisons between countries and over time, this metric is [age-standardized](#dod:age_standardized).
caption:
Incidence rate of cardiovascular diseases

Estimated share of the population who newly develop cardiovascular diseases, in a given year.

This choropleth world map displays the 2023 incidence rate of cardiovascular diseases, representing the estimated share of the population who newly develop these conditions in a given year. The metric is age-standardized to allow for comparisons between countries and over time. The legend ranges from 0.5% to 0.9%, with color intensity increasing from pale yellow to dark red. The highest incidence rates, falling within the 0.8% to 0.9% range indicated by dark orange and red shades, are concentrated in Eastern Europe and parts of the Middle East. Specifically, Russia, Ukraine, and Egypt appear in the darkest bands on the map. Surrounding these areas, much of Central Asia and North Africa display mid-range orange shades corresponding to values between 0.6% and 0.8%. In contrast, large portions of South America, including Argentina and Chile, along with Australia and parts of Western Europe, are shaded in the lightest yellow tones, indicating rates near the 0.5% threshold. North America and China generally sit in the light-to-mid range of the spectrum. Several countries in central Africa and Greenland appear in grey with diagonal hatching, denoting that no data is available for these regions. The visual distribution highlights a distinct geographic variation, with the highest shares of new cases clustered in specific Eurasian and North African regions, while the lowest shares are observed across the Southern Hemisphere and parts of Western Europe.
